title: My School Bag
summary: A young girl describes what is inside her school bag and how she uses each item at school every day.
body: I have a blue school bag. It is not very big, but it holds a lot of things. Every morning I pack my bag before school. I put in my pencil case first. Inside the pencil case, I have pencils, a rubber, and a ruler. I also have two notebooks. One notebook is for maths and one is for English. I have a reading book too. My teacher gives us new books every month. I always carry a water bottle in my bag. It is important to drink water at school. I also have my lunch box. Today I have a sandwich and an apple inside. At the bottom of my bag, I keep my bus pass. I need it to travel to school and back. My bag is heavy on some days. But I love my school bag because my mum chose it for me.

annotations.exampleEn: She packed her school bag the night before.
This bag holds a lot — you can put books, a laptop, and snacks in it.
I pack my bag every night so I am ready in the morning.
Her pencil case has a zipper and holds many pens.
He used a rubber to erase the wrong answer.
Draw a straight line with a ruler.
She wrote all her notes in a small notebook.
Don't forget to take your water bottle to the gym.

annotations.meaningVi: hộp cơm, hộp đựng bữa trưa
annotations.exampleEn: She opened her lunch box and found a note from her mum.
He had a cheese sandwich for lunch.
She showed her bus pass to the driver.
The bag is too heavy — she can barely lift it.

grammarNotes.title: Possessive adjectives: my, your, his, her
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Tính từ sở hữu cho biết vật gì thuộc về ai. Trong bài này: 'my bag', 'my pencil case', 'my teacher', 'my mum'. 'My' nghĩa là 'của tôi'. Những tính từ sở hữu phổ biến: my (của tôi), your (của bạn), his (của anh ấy), her (của cô ấy), our (của chúng tôi), their (của họ). Chúng đứng trước danh từ.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Possessive adjectives show who something belongs to. In this passage: 'my bag', 'my pencil case', 'my teacher', 'my mum'. 'My' means it belongs to the speaker. Common possessive adjectives: my, your, his, her, our, their. They always come before a noun.

grammarNotes.title: Also – Adding more information
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Also' nghĩa là 'cũng, thêm vào đó'. Nó được dùng để thêm một thông tin nữa vào câu: 'I also have two notebooks.' / 'I also have my lunch box.' 'Also' thường đứng trước động từ chính. Nó khác với 'too' — 'too' thường đứng cuối câu: 'I have a lunch box too.'
grammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Also' means 'in addition'. It is used to add another piece of information: 'I also have two notebooks.' / 'I also have my lunch box.' 'Also' usually goes before the main verb. It differs from 'too', which typically appears at the end of a sentence: 'I have a lunch box too.'

translationVi: Tôi có một cái cặp sách màu xanh. Nó không to lắm nhưng chứa được nhiều thứ. Mỗi buổi sáng tôi sắp xếp đồ vào cặp trước khi đến trường. Trước tiên tôi cho hộp bút vào. Trong hộp bút có bút chì, cục tẩy và thước kẻ. Tôi cũng có hai cuốn vở. Một cuốn để học toán và một cuốn để học tiếng Anh. Tôi cũng có một quyển sách đọc. Giáo viên của tôi phát sách mới cho chúng tôi mỗi tháng. Tôi luôn mang theo một chai nước trong cặp. Uống nước ở trường rất quan trọng. Tôi cũng có hộp cơm của mình. Hôm nay bên trong có một cái bánh mì kẹp và một quả táo. Ở đáy cặp, tôi để thẻ xe buýt. Tôi cần nó để đi học và về nhà. Có những ngày cặp tôi rất nặng. Nhưng tôi yêu cái cặp của mình vì mẹ đã chọn nó cho tôi.

questions.prompt: What colour is the narrator's school bag?
questions.explanation: The passage says 'I have a blue school bag.'
questions.hint: What colour does the narrator say her bag is?

questions.prompt: When does she pack her bag?
questions.explanation: The passage says 'Every morning I pack my bag before school.'
questions.hint: What time of day does she prepare her bag?

questions.prompt: What three items are inside the pencil case?
questions.explanation: The passage says 'Inside the pencil case, I have pencils, a rubber, and a ruler.'
questions.hint: Name the three tools inside her pencil case.

questions.prompt: What is one notebook used for?
questions.explanation: The passage says 'One notebook is for maths and one is for English.'
questions.hint: The passage names two subjects for her notebooks.

questions.prompt: How often does the teacher give new reading books?
questions.explanation: The passage says 'My teacher gives us new books every month.'
questions.hint: How frequently does the teacher hand out books?

questions.prompt: Why does she always carry a water bottle?
options: Because it is important to drink water at school
Because her mum told her to
questions.explanation: The passage says 'It is important to drink water at school.'
questions.hint: What reason does she give for carrying water?

questions.prompt: What is in her lunch box today?
questions.explanation: The passage says 'Today I have a sandwich and an apple inside.'
questions.hint: Name the two food items in today's lunch box.

questions.prompt: Where does she keep her bus pass?
options: At the top of her bag
At the bottom of her bag
questions.explanation: The passage says 'At the bottom of my bag, I keep my bus pass.'
questions.hint: Which part of her bag holds the bus pass?

questions.prompt: What does she use the bus pass for?
options: To travel to school and back
questions.explanation: The passage says 'I need it to travel to school and back.'
questions.hint: What is the bus pass needed for?

questions.prompt: Why does she love her school bag?
options: Because it is a famous brand
Because her mum chose it for her
Because it is the biggest bag at school
questions.explanation: The passage says 'I love my school bag because my mum chose it for me.'
questions.hint: Who picked out the bag for her?

questions.prompt: How many notebooks does the narrator have?
questions.explanation: The passage says 'I also have two notebooks.'
questions.hint: Count the notebooks mentioned in the passage.

cloze.template: I have a blue school {{b1}}. It is not very big, but it holds a lot of things. Every morning I pack my bag before school. I put in my pencil case first. Inside the pencil case, I have pencils, a {{b2}}, and a ruler. I also have two {{b3}}. One notebook is for {{b4}} and one is for English. I have a reading book too. My teacher gives us new books every {{b5}}. I always carry a water {{b6}} in my bag. It is important to drink water at school. I also have my lunch {{b7}}. Today I have a sandwich and an {{b8}} inside. At the bottom of my bag, I keep my bus {{b9}}. I need it to travel to school and back. My bag is {{b10}} on some days. But I love my school bag because my mum chose it for me.

blanks.explanation: 'School bag' — the item the whole passage is describing.
'A rubber' is a British English word for an eraser, used to remove pencil marks.
'Two notebooks' — one for maths and one for English.
'One notebook is for maths' — maths is the first subject named.
'Every month' — the frequency with which the teacher gives new reading books.
'Water bottle' — a reusable container for drinking water.
'Lunch box' — a container for carrying a packed lunch to school.
'An apple' — one of the two food items in her lunch box today.
'Bus pass' — a card or permit that allows her to travel by bus.
'Heavy on some days' — when she carries all her books, the bag becomes difficult to carry.

criticalThinkingQuestion: The narrator says her bag is heavy on some days. Do you think carrying a heavy school bag every day could be a problem for children? What could schools or families do to help?
